title: Glossostipula blepharophylla
intro: Glossostipula blepharophylla, also known as Blepharophylla, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Glossostipula blepharophylla include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and flavonoids, such as blepharophylline and ursolic acid, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Glossostipula blepharophylla is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory infections and other health conditions.

The leaves and stems of Glossostipula blepharophylla are often harvested for medicinal purposes. However, specific information on the parts used for medicinal purposes is not widely available.
